name: Malaysian Hot Noodles with Tofu
recipeIngredient: 1 bn Choy sum leaves and stalks, chopped into 1-inch sections
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak dried tofu slices in hot water for 15 minutes.
2. When softened, cut into 1-inch squares, drain, and pat dry.
3. Cook the wheat noodles in boiling water until al dente; a little firm. Drain, rinse under cold water, and drain again.
4. Heat oil in a wok over high heat, and deep-fry the tofu cubes until golden brown. Remove from pan and drain.
5. Next, deep-fry the squares of dried tofu until golden brown and slightly blistered; remove and drain.
6. Heat sesame oil in another wok on full heat; saute the minced ginger for 1 minute.
7. Add asafoetida and chopped choy sum, and stir fry until soft.
8. Add soy sauce, sambal oelek, lemon juice, tofu noodles. Stir fry for another 2 minutes or until the noodles are hot.
9. Serve immediately.
asafoetida powder: available at Indian grocers
choy sum: also known as rape
sambal oelek: a hot condiment made from ground fresh red, hot chilies, popular in Malay and Indonesian cuisine. Available at Asian grocery stores.
